mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3, blocking oncogenic signaling
additional_notes: Reported in vitro AML activity; potent multi‑kinase TKI.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL family kinases and other tyrosine kinases, affecting signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Broad kinase inhibitor; reported activity and potential for synergy with other AML agents.

mechanism: Inhibits several receptor tyrosine kinases to impair tumor cell signaling
additional_notes: Some activity reported in AML cell lines; multi‑target profile.

mechanism: Inhibits RET, VEGFR, MET and other kinases to block proliferative and survival pathways
additional_notes: Reported AML cytotoxicity in some lines; multi‑kinase targeting.

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Proteasome inhibition can be cytotoxic to AML cells; activity reported in AML models.

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome, causing proteotoxic stress and cell death
additional_notes: More potent proteasome inhibitor with in vitro AML activity.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, increasing oxidative stress and inducing cell death
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with reported sub‑µM to low‑µM IC50s in AML. If very active, consider repeating with top 1 µM.

mechanism: Modulates multiple cancer‑relevant pathways (Wnt/β‑catenin, STAT3, mitochondrial uncoupling, etc.) to reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Reported to reduce AML proliferation at low‑µM concentrations.

mechanism: Forms cytotoxic complexes with copper (Cu) and generates oxidative/protein‑modifying species; copper‑dependent cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Test with 1 µM CuCl2 (maintain same copper in all wells) for activity; without copper expect reduced effect.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase, affecting cholesterol synthesis and prenylation pathways that can reduce proliferation
additional_notes: Reported antiproliferative effects on AML at µM levels; lipophilicity may require formulation checks.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism (complex I inhibition, AMPK activation) leading to antiproliferative effects at high concentrations
additional_notes: Requires high (mM) concentrations in vitro; interpret results cautiously and consider combination testing.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, altering chromatin and transcription to induce cell cycle arrest and apoptosis
additional_notes: HDAC inhibition can induce cell death in AML models; often tested in combination strategies.

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2, blocking MAPK/ERK signaling downstream of RAS/RAF
additional_notes: Can synergize with other agents in AML; if no effect at 100 nM, consider repeating with top 1 µM.
